joanna liu
about medesignprojects

projects

Below are some game development projects I’ve worked on in my past years of college. If you’d like to jump right into playing any games I’ve made, head on over to my itch.io page. If you’re interested in coding projects, I’d recommend you check out my github.

2024 - 'lotlneck

My Roles: art, ui/ux, design.

Software: procreate, unity.

I worked in a trio to create this simple instance runner (with a moral-dilemma twist) for our club's website jam. You can try playing 'LOTLNECK (a combination of axolotl and bottleneck) in-browser here!

title art for our game

The theme of the game jam was "Break the Cycle", and after a while of brainstorming, our team thought it would be fun to include the concept of a trolley problem in the game.

rough sketches of the train with markings for tiling

concept for the bar cart and screen shape

cleaned vision for our game

testing different color schemes for the background

concepts and final drafts for the buff and debuff icons

some gameplay!
2024 - (over)lord of the flies

My roles: art, tech art, ui/ux, design.

Software: photoshop, procreate, unity.

This was a game I made for LD56 with a couple of friends in an effort to beat out some of our other friends in a friendly competition with the LD56 rankings. Safe to say, I love how our game ended up looking! You can play it here, and check out our LD56 submission here.

title screen I designed
We wanted to make the art-style a unique, and I wanted to try out some different styles, so we ended up going with a hybrid of collaging and sketchy-art. I did all of the collaging in Photoshop.

sprites I made in photoshop

a few menu designs that I made

gameplay
2024 - glowshade

My roles: project manager, art dirctor, tech art, game design.

Software: unreal engine 5, figma, procreate.

This game was the first time I've led a game project team, and also my first time working in Unreal Engine. It was definitely a challenge with ups and downs, but I'm pretty happy with the demo we were able to complete in the two quarters. If you're interested in playing, you can download it here!

We started out with just the notion of 2.5D platformer that has light-based platforms, and the rest of the game built itself from there.

Aside from team management and game directing, I also helped with the art and visual design for the game, some of which I've put below!

original concepts for the gameplay and mood

player character concepts and npc concepts

concepts for the title text

one of the original concepts for the title screen

I also helped with the level design and scoping of our project. We did a lot of our planning and outlining in FigJam, as shown below. Click to enlarge the images.

outline/flowchart of gameplay

level design sketches in FigJam
×

Though we weren't able to get all of the programming in place by the end of the quarter like we'd hoped, I think the overall aesthetic that we were trying to create was well captured! Though the following gifs don't do the quality justice, below are some quick gameplay clips.

some platform toggling in game

the beginning of the factory level
2023 - on my own (omo)

My roles: art, ui/ux.

Software: procreate, unity, figma.

OMO was my first long-term game project experience. I mainly helped with UI, game design, story, and art. Below is some UI and concept art I made for the game.

ui menus and buttons for omo

omo concept art
